rationale: Empirical prober lens: I ran every factual claim in this tree and all hold. `go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep -c internal/config` = 0 — R8's config-free-leaf invariant is true today, so 'internal/panel imports no internal/config' is verified, not aspirational. ApproveThreshold IS at panel.go:76 with exactly the ExpectedReviewers<=0->0 / else N-1 semantics R7 describes, and the pre-existing N=1=>0 edge the spec scopes out is real. config.go's DefaultConfig inline-literal + Load DefaultConfig-prepopulate-then-Unmarshal + per-field zero-value backfill + fmt.Errorf(...:%w) conventions all exist as the spec claims to extend them. ADR dir highest is 0039, so 0040 is genuinely next free. All eight cited ADRs' Domain(s) lines match the spec verbatim; ADR-0037/0034 are both Status: Accepted, grounding the 0040-Accepted precedent. `mindspec validate spec 109` emits only the expected lifecycle-binding WARN (exit 0). The threshold-skip defense is coherently double-homed (config-side [1,sum] in R5, record-side [1,N] in R7) with no contradiction. Spec is falsifiable, repo-grounded, and internally consistent.
findings: VERIFIED (R8): `go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep -c internal/config` returns 0; no config import in the full dep list. The 'dependency-clean leaf' claim is empirically true in the reviewed commit.

VERIFIED (R7): internal/panel/panel.go:76 is `func (p Panel) ApproveThreshold() int`; body returns 0 when ExpectedReviewers<=0 else ExpectedReviewers-1. The spec's line reference and N-1 single-home semantics are accurate, and the out-of-scope N=1=>0 absent-field edge exists as stated.

VERIFIED (R2/R3/R4): config.go DefaultConfig() is an inline *Config literal; loadUncached does cfg:=DefaultConfig() then yaml.Unmarshal, with a PanelGate comment (lines 65-67) documenting that yaml.v3 leaves pre-populated fields untouched for absent keys, plus explicit len==0 / ==0 per-field backfill. The spec's zero-config round-trip and default-via-DefaultConfig style match existing convention.
VERIFIED (R1): `ls .mindspec/adr/ | sort | tail` shows ADR-0039 as the highest; no ADR-0040 exists, so 0040 is the correct next-free number for Bead 1.

VERIFIED (ADR Touchpoints): Domain(s) lines match verbatim for all eight cited ADRs (0037 workflow,execution; 0035 workflow,execution,core; 0034 workflow; 0036 workflow,validation,doc-sync,ownership; 0039 core,workflow,execution,context-system; 0032 validation,adr,lifecycle,workflow; 0023 workflow,git,state; 0038 core,execution,workflow). ADR-0037 and ADR-0034 both carry `- **Status**: Accepted`, validating the 'codification ADRs land Accepted (precedent 0034/0037)' resolution.

VERIFIED (lifecycle): `mindspec validate spec 109-orchestration-config-substrate` returns exit 0 with exactly one issue: [WARN] lifecycle-binding (no beads epic yet) — the expected pre-approve state, no ERRORs.

VERIFIED (consistency): R5 (config-side approve_threshold in [1, sum(reviewers.count)]) and R7 (record-side integer in [1, ExpectedReviewers], out-of-range falls back to N-1) are complementary defense-in-depth, not contradictory; a recorded 0 can never become an always-pass on either side.

SOFT (non-blocking): R7 hardcodes the literal line reference 'panel.go:76'. It is exact today, but a line number can drift before implementation; the method name is also given, so this is cosmetic and does not warrant a change.
